---
description: "Use when writing or updating website copy, content structure, and metadata for game pages, studio pages, and contact sections. Enforces consistent voice and conversion-focused messaging."
name: "Content Authoring Guidelines"
applyTo: "**/*.{html,md,mdx,txt}, **/content/**/*.{json,yml,yaml}"
---

# Studio Website Content Guidelines

Use this instruction when creating or editing website content.

## Primary Goals

- Help visitors discover and understand the studio games.
- Build trust by communicating who the studio is and what it values.
- Make contacting the studio straightforward with clear calls to action.

## Information Architecture Baseline

- Home should include: clear studio value proposition, featured games, short studio intro, and contact call to action.
- Games area should include: game cards plus detailed pages or sections for each title.
- About area should include: studio mission, team story, and development philosophy.
- Contact area should include: form intent, expected response time, and alternate contact method.

## Writing Style

- Use clear, direct, active voice.
- Keep paragraphs short and scannable.
- Avoid vague hype language without specific details.
- Prefer player-facing benefits over technical jargon when writing marketing-facing copy.

## Game Entry Content Requirements

- Game title
- One-sentence pitch
- Genre and platform targets
- Development status
- Primary call to action (wishlist, play demo, follow updates, or equivalent)

## Contact Content Requirements

- Form purpose statement (what kinds of messages are welcome)
- Required field labels that are explicit and unambiguous
- Confirmation or success message text
- Brief privacy statement about how submitted information is used

## SEO And Accessibility Content Rules

- Provide unique page titles and meta descriptions for major pages.
- Keep heading hierarchy logical and sequential.
- Ensure image alt text is descriptive and contextual, not keyword stuffing.
- Ensure link text describes destination or action without relying on context alone.

## Validation Checklist

- Content supports the games, studio, and contact goals
- Calls to action are visible and specific
- Metadata is present for key pages
- Content is readable on mobile and desktop
